data.summary: Exploratory evidence is insufficient to assert a hard requirement failure.
data.assessment: The exploratory step aimed to validate the requirement of 'Hybrid participation parity (remote can ask questions, vote)', but the evidence does not confirm that remote participants can actively engage in asking questions or voting. The linked use case mentions that 'hand-raising works both ways' and 'polls include all participants', but it lacks explicit confirmation of the mechanisms for remote participants to ask questions and vote in real-time. Therefore, there is a gap in meeting the requirement as the evidence does not provide sufficient clarity on remote participation capabilities.
gaps: The evidence does not explicitly confirm the mechanisms for remote participants to ask questions and vote.

deterministicFollowUps.title: Add explicit API endpoints for remote participation
deterministicFollowUps.assertion: Define concrete API calls that verify remote participants can ask questions and vote.
